channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Heavy snow brings widespread disruption across the UK and Germany
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Heavy snow and freezing rain brought widespread disruption across Europe on Sunday, particularly in the U.K. and Germany, with several major airports forced to suspend flights. With the weather set to stay inclement on Sunday in the U.K.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS and MONIKA SCISLOWSKA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Shigemi Fukahori, who survived Nagasaki bombing and spent his life advocating for peace, dies at 93
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Shigemi Fukahori, a survivor of the 1945 Nagasaki atomic bombing, who devoted his life to advocating for peace and campaigning against nuclear weapons, has died. He was 93. Fukahori died at a hospital in Nagasaki, southwestern Japan, on Jan.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Italy's Meloni makes surprise Mar-a-Lago visit to meet with Trump
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni made a surprise visit to Mar-a-Lago to meet with President-elect Trump on Saturday. They were joined by a number of Trump’s expected nominees for his forthcoming administration, according to a pool report, including Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Austrian nationalist party leader rumored to be in talks to form government
item.description: Herbert Kickl, leader of Austria's right-wing Freedom Party, met with President Alexander Van der Bellen on Sunday, further fueling rumors that he will be asked to form a government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A soccer-loving nun from Brazil tops list of world's oldest living person at nearly 117
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — A soccer-loving nun from Brazil is believed to have become the world's oldest living person at nearly 117 following the recent death of a woman from Japan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean protesters call for President Yoon’s arrest as deadline nears
item.description: Protesters in South Korea are calling for President Yoon Suk Yeol to be arrested as a warrant expires at midnight on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Speculation grows that Austrian far right leader Herbert Kickl will be asked to form a government
item.description: Austrian President Alexander Van der Bellen says he will meet with far-right politician Herbert Kickl as speculation grows that he will ask the Freedom Party leader to form a government.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Liechtenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel helps former soldier leave Brazil over investigation into alleged war crimes in Gaza
item.description: Israel has helped a former soldier leave Brazil after legal action was initiated against him by a group accusing Israelis of war crimes in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Sam Mednick And Wafaa Shurafa, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Costas Simitis, former Greek prime minister and socialist leader, dies at 88
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — Costas Simitis, former prime minister of Greece and the architect of the country’s joining the common European currency, the euro, has died at age 88, state TV ERT reported.
item.dc:creator: DEMETRIS NELLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean protesters brave cold to demand Yoon's ouster as detention deadline looms
item.description: Hundreds of South Koreans, bundled up against freezing temperatures and snow, rallied overnight near the residence of impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol, calling for his ouster and arrest, as authorities prepared to renew their efforts to detain him over his short-lived martial law decree.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israeli airstrikes in Gaza kill at least 21, hospital workers say, as talks underway
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes killed at least 21 people, including at least two children, on Saturday in Gaza, hospital staff said, while a new effort at ceasefire talks was underway in Qatar after nearly 15 months of war.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World’s oldest person dies in Japan at 116
item.description: Tomiko Itooka, the oldest person in the world, has died at 116. She was born in Osaka, Japan, in 1908 and is survived by one son, one daughter and five grandchildren.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Orleans attack puts focus on Trump’s counter-ISIS strategy
item.description: The deadly New Orleans attack on New Year’s Day, which authorities say was carried out by a man radicalized by ISIS, is spurring fears of more attacks and raising questions about how President-elect Trump’s isolationist tendencies will affect the war on terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Trump announces Middle East appointment: Hopefully she's learned her lesson
item.description: President-elect Trump tapped Morgan Ortagus, a former State Department spokesperson, to serve as deputy special presidential envoy for Middle East peace in his next administration. Trump noted in his announcement that Ortagus previously was critical of him.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Greenland leader calls for independence from Denmark as Trump enters
item.description: Greenland Prime Minister Múte Egede announced a desire to obtain independence from Denmark in the wake of President-elect Trump’s comments about buying the island territory.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Bipartisan lawmakers renew push for legislation supporting Georgia against Russian interference
item.description: Republican and Democratic lawmakers are renewing efforts to pass legislation supporting Georgia, touting it as one of the best and first examples of bipartisan unity of the new Congress. Reps. Joe Wilson (R-S.C.) and Steve Cohen (D-Tenn.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Montenegro to tackle gun control after mass killing left 12 dead
item.description: PODGORICA, Montenegro (AP) — A top-level meeting in Montenegro on Friday promised tough measures to curb illegal weapons after a gunman fatally shot 12 people in a second such tragedy in less than three years in the small Balkan country.
item.dc:creator: PREDRAG MILIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 30 people killed by Israeli strikes in Gaza
item.description: At least 30 people, including children, were killed in Gaza by Israeli strikes overnight and into Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Iran executes over 1K prisoners in 2024, highest total in 30 years, report says
item.description: A new report claimed Iran executed an unprecedented number of people in 2024. Most of the executions took place following the election of its new president in late July.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ISIS increasingly unopposed following US withdrawal from Afghanistan, collapse of Syria
item.description: The Islamic State is seeing less resistance in areas of major security concern following the U.S. withdrawal from Afghanistan, the fall of the Assad regime in Syria and potential troop drawdown in Iraq.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Zealand police officer killed in New Year's Day vehicle attack, another injured
item.description: A driver ran down two New Zealand police officers on patrol in the early hours of New Year’s Day, killing one and seriously injuring the other, the country’s police chief said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bangladesh court again rejects bail for Hindu leader who led rallies
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — A court in southeastern Bangladesh on Thursday rejected a plea for bail by a jailed Hindu leader who led large rallies in the Muslim-majority country demanding better security for minority groups.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An armed man kills at least 10 people, including 2 children, in a shooting rampage in Montenegro
item.description: PODGORICA, Montenegro (AP) — At least 10 people, including two children, were killed and four others were seriously wounded on Wednesday in a shooting rampage that followed a bar brawl in a western Montenegrin city, officials said. The shooter was on the run.
item.dc:creator: PREDRAG MILIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope calls for commitment to protect life as he doubles down on abortion in New Year's Day message
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis ushered in the New Year with a renewed appeal for the faithful to reject abortion, calling for a “firm commitment” to protect and respect life from conception to natural death. Francis, 88, celebrated a New Year’s Day Mass in St.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea air crash investigators extract black box data as grieving families mourn the victims
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Grieving relatives of the victims of the South Korea plane crash gathered at the site to pay respects to their loved ones on New Year’s Day, as officials said they've extracted data from one of the retrieved black boxes to find the exact cause of the crash.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Arrest warrant issued for impeached South Korean president as political crisis deepens
item.description: A South Korean court approved arrest warrants on Tuesday to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol and search his office and residence amid a criminal investigation into his martial law decree.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Court issues arrest warrant for South Korea's President Yoon
item.description: A South Korean court issues arrest warrant for sitting President Yoon Suk Yeol over his decision in December to impose martial law over the nation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jetliner skids off runway and bursts into flames while landing in South Korea, killing 179
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A jetliner skidded off a runway, slammed into a concrete fence and burst into flames Sunday in South Korea after its landing gear apparently failed to deploy. All but two of the 181 people aboard were killed in one of the country’s worst aviation disasters, officials said.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation
